channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Power Pair: The rabbi and the politician
item.description: Rabbi Marcia Zimmerman, the senior rabbi of Minnesota’s largest synagogue, and Frank Hornstein, a former representative in the Minnesota House, have been married for 42 years. Coming up at 9 a.m.
item.dc:creator: Angela Davis and Maja Beckstrom, MPR News

item.title: Ethics watchdog flags senator helping wife rake in millions for nonprofit
item.description: Sen. Sheldon Whitehouse is being accused of a potential conflict of interest after his wife's firm received money from an organization given grants by the federal government.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: New study finds elevated cancer rates near toxic San Jacinto River waste pits
item.description: State researchers found above normal levels of four types of cancer in communities near a toxic waste site.
item.dc:creator: Texas Tribune, By Alejandra Martinez, KPRC 2

item.title: Texas House unveils plan to cut property taxes, setting up fight with Senate
item.description: Lawmakers will debate how to spend $3.5 billion in proposed tax cuts, a legislative priority for Republican leaders.
item.dc:creator: Texas Tribune, By Joshua Fechter, KPRC 2

item.title: Trump defends Musk’s ‘genius’ email to federal workers
item.description: President Trump on Monday defended tech billionaire Elon Musk after pushback over his email demanding federal workers list five accomplishments from the past week and in doing so, raising the of risk losing their jobs. “There was a lot of genius in sending it.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Legal expert: Federal employee safeguards moving too slowly to protect workers
item.description: Federal employees filed a lawsuit over an email telling them to list their accomplishments from the last week and a warning from Elon Musk that if they do not respond, they could lose their jobs.
item.dc:creator: Nina Moini and Alanna Elder, MPR News

item.title: Elon Musk's underground digital detective reveals all to NewsNation
item.description: NewsNation's Brian Entin has the first interview with the tech genius using AI to discover alleged government waste and corruption.
item.dc:creator: Brian Entin, NewsNation

item.title: Ex-Secret Service agent and conservative media personality Dan Bongino picked as FBI deputy director
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Dan Bongino, a former U.S. Secret Service agent who ran unsuccessfully for office and gained fame as a conservative pundit with TV shows and a popular podcast, has been chosen to serve as FBI deputy director.
item.dc:creator: ADRIANA GOMEZ LICON and ERIC TUCKER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: New FBI Director Kash Patel is sworn in as acting ATF chief, AP source says
item.description: New FBI Director Kash Patel was sworn in Monday as acting chief of the federal bureau of Alcohol, Tobacco, Firearms and Explosives, taking the helm of two separate and sprawling Justice Department agencies, according to a person familiar with the matter.
item.dc:creator: Alanna Durkin Richer, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Republican lawmaker files bills to ban DEI in Texas K-12 public schools
item.description: Sen. Brandon Creighton has filed bills that would ban diversity, equity and inclusion policies related to hiring and programming, while creating ways for parents to complain about violations to the DEI ban.
item.dc:creator: Texas Tribune, By Jaden Edison, KPRC 2

item.title: Supreme Court turns down chance to claw back abortion clinic buffer zones
item.description: The Supreme Court turned down an opportunity to overturn its precedent permitting buffer zones around abortion clinics over the objections of two of the court’s leading conservatives. In two orders issued Monday, the court declined to take up challenges to ordinances in Carbondale, Ill.
item.dc:creator: Zach Schonfeld, NewsNation

item.title: Supreme Court turns back challenges to laws keeping abortion opponents away from clinics, patients
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Supreme Court refused Monday to hear a pair of cases from abortion opponents who say laws limiting anti-abortion demonstrations near clinics violate their First Amendment rights.
item.dc:creator: LINDSAY WHITEHURST, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: DOGE claims it has saved billions, but nearly 75% unaccounted for
item.description: DOGE hasn't provided evidence for approximately $38 billion in alleged savings — nearly 75% of its total claim.
item.dc:creator: Tom Dempsey, NewsNation

item.title: In window before special election, House Republicans push for votes on their agenda
item.description: With a slim majority, Republicans in the Minnesota House are pushing forward bills that are likely not to pass a floor vote. They hope it will still send a political message.
item.dc:creator: Peter Cox, MPR News

item.title: US votes against UN resolution demanding Russia withdraw its forces from Ukraine
item.description: In a dramatic shift in transatlantic relations under President Donald Trump, the United States has split with its European allies by refusing to blame Russia for its invasion of Ukraine in votes on three U.N. resolutions seeking an end to the three-year war.
item.dc:creator: Edith M. Lederer, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Trump expresses hope Russia's war in Ukraine is nearing an endgame as he meets with France's Macron
item.description: President Donald Trump says he's hopeful Russia's war in Ukraine is nearing an endgame as he meets with French President Emmanuel Macron on the third anniversary of the Russian invasion.
item.dc:creator: Matthew Lee And Aamer Madhani, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: The Trump administration is putting USAID staffers on leave worldwide and firing at least 1,600
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Trump administration moved its fast-paced dismantling of the U.S. Agency for International Development toward what appeared to be its final phases, telling all but a fraction of staffers worldwide that they were on leave as of Monday and notifying at least 1,600 of the U.S.
item.dc:creator: ELLEN KNICKMEYER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Hegseth defends Trump's firings of Pentagon leaders and says there may be more dismissals
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth insists President Donald Trump ’s abrupt firing of the nation’s senior military officer amid a wave of dismissals at the Pentagon wasn't unusual, brushing aside outcry that the new administration is openly seeking to inject politics into the military.
item.dc:creator: WILL WEISSERT, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Musk gives all federal workers 48 hours to explain what they did last week or face consequences
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Hundreds of thousands of federal workers have been given little more than 48 hours to explain what they accomplished over the last week, sparking confusion across key agencies as billionaire Elon Musk expands his drive to slash the size of federal government.
item.dc:creator: STEVE PEOPLES, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Meet five Minnesota federal workers mourning the loss of their dream jobs
item.description: Many Minnesotans are among those facing unemployment, as the Trump administration slashes jobs across agencies. The state is home to more than 18,000 federal workers, with nearly 60 percent working for the Department of Veterans Affairs and the U.S. Department of Agriculture.
item.dc:creator: MPR News Staff, MPR News

item.title: NIH funding freeze stalls applications on $1.5 billion in medical research funds
item.description: The National Institutes of Health had to stop considering new grant applications, delaying funding for research into diseases ranging from heart disease and cancer to Alzheimer’s and allergies.
item.dc:creator: Rob Stein, MPR News

item.title: Federal judge temporarily blocks Trump DEI executive orders
item.description: U.S. District Judge Adam B. Abelson, a former President Biden appointee, issued a preliminary injunction on Friday.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, NewsNation

item.title: PIX Panel discusses Hochul’s safeguard plan for NYC
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) – Gov. Kathy Hochul on Thursday announced proposed safeguards to ensure that New York City is operating in the best interest of its residents.
item.dc:creator: Dan Mannarino, PIX 11

item.title: The Supreme Court won’t allow Trump to immediately fire head of whistleblower office
item.description: The Supreme Court on Friday temporarily kept on the job the head of the federal agency that protects government whistleblowers, in its first word on the many legal fights over President Donald Trump’s second-term agenda.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Lawmakers to Trump: Reverse cuts to 9/11 health care
item.description: New York's senate and congressional delegations agree that federal cuts to the World Trade Center Health Program will hurt survivors of and first responders to the Sept. 11 terrorist attacks.
item.dc:creator: Johan Sheridan, PIX 11

item.title: Nevada Democrats look to enshrine right to IVF in state law
item.description: Nevada Democrats introduced a proposal Wednesday to create a state law protecting the right to in vitro fertilization (IVF).
item.dc:creator: David Charns, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Florida cabinet assumes new role, sparking criticism over privacy and power
item.description: After a month-long feud over who would be the state's chief immigration enforcement officer, state lawmakers passed a bill package that grants not one, but four elected officials with that task.
item.dc:creator: Mackenzie LaPorte, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Trump signals 25 percent tariffs coming on cars, semiconductors and pharmaceuticals
item.description: President Trump on Tuesday indicated he would unveil tariffs on imports of automobiles, semiconductors and pharmaceuticals in early April, starting at 25 percent.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, WFLA, NBC
